summary: Most requested frontend changes for a browser-playable Tic-Tac-Toe screen are implemented (board UI, random computer moves, restart, header update). However, the win-detection logic appears incorrect for one diagonal, and the current turn/effect flow can still allow a computer move immediately after a human winning move due to state update timing.

missing_requirements.requirement: Detect wins across rows, columns, and diagonals for both 'O' and 'X' consistent with the provided Python victory logic.
missing_requirements.reason: The anti-diagonal check appears to be implemented incorrectly: `cross2` checks `board[2 - rc][2 - rc]` which is the same diagonal as `cross1` (main diagonal). This likely fails to detect wins on the other diagonal.

missing_requirements.requirement: After the game ends, no further moves can be made until restarting.
missing_requirements.reason: The computer-move effect can run after a human move even if that human move ends the game, because `setIsComputerTurn(true)` occurs immediately and the effect checks `gameStatus === 'playing'` based on the prior render; `gameStatus` is updated in a separate effect after `board` changes, so a computer move may still be scheduled/applied right after a winning human move.
evidence: frontend/src/features/tictactoe/TicTacToeGame.tsx (handleCellClick sets isComputerTurn=true after setting board; computer move useEffect gates on gameStatus state)

hallucinated_features.description: Footer adds caffeine.ai attribution link with dynamic UTM tracking based on hostname (`appIdentifier`).
hallucinated_features.reason: Not requested by the BuildRequest; the request only mentions updating the app shell header text while keeping layout structure.
evidence: frontend/src/features/layout/AppShell.tsx (appIdentifier, caffeine.ai link with UTM params)

hallucinated_features.description: Introduced a 500ms delay before the computer makes its move.
hallucinated_features.reason: Not requested; the BuildRequest only requires that the computer move happens automatically and randomly after the human move.
